name: Reproduce Paper
description: Reproduce an ML paper from a paper URL, repository, or method description with evidence-backed metrics.

inputs.description: Paper URL, arXiv identifier, or Hugging Face Papers link.
Optional code repository or implementation reference.
Primary metric or paper table result to reproduce.

permissions.description: Read linked papers, public repositories, model cards, and dataset cards.

approval_points.description: Start the budgeted reproduction or baseline training run.
Publish or share reproduced artifacts outside the local workspace.

phases.name: Intake
phases.objective: Capture paper target, available code, success metric, and reproduction constraints.

phases.name: Paper Extraction
phases.objective: Extract method, datasets, splits, preprocessing, baselines, metrics, and claimed results.

phases.name: Environment And Data
phases.objective: Prepare reproducible environment and dataset revision or fingerprint before experiments.

phases.name: Baseline Run
phases.objective: Run a baseline or sanity check before reproducing the target method.

phases.name: Reproduction Run
phases.objective: Run the target implementation and record metrics, configuration, logs, and artifacts.

phases.name: Verification
phases.objective: Compare observed results against the paper and verify evidence for final claims.

required_outputs.description: Goal, target result, constraints, and available assets.
Claimed paper metrics, datasets, and baselines.
Runtime, dependency, hardware, and seed snapshot.
Dataset source, revision, splits, and fingerprint.
Experiment command, config, stdout, stderr, and exit status.

verifiers.description: The target metric, dataset, and comparison claim are specific enough to verify.
Extracted claims cite the paper section, table, repository, or model card source.
Dataset source, revision, split mapping, and fingerprint are recorded.
Baseline metric exists and is compared with the reproduction result.
Target metric is present with units, split, and run identifier.
Environment, dependency versions, hardware class, and seeds are captured.
Final report claims are supported by recorded metrics, logs, and source citations.
